claim: DeepSWE benchmark is contamination-free (tasks written from scratch, not from public GitHub issues) so models haven't seen solutions during training
actionable: Use DeepSWE for evaluating coding models when you need reliable benchmarks not gamed by pre-training data overlap
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...

claim: DeepSWE prompts are short (half the length of SWE-bench) but require 5.5x more code to solve, better reflecting real developer interaction
actionable: Test coding agents with realistic short prompts instead of over-specified problem statements; this reveals true reasoning capability
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...

claim: SWE-bench Pro has 8% false positive and 24% false negative rates in its verifier; DeepSWE reduced these to 0.3% and 1.1% respectively
actionable: Don't trust SWE-bench Pro's leaderboard results; use DeepSWE for more reliable model rankings on coding tasks
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...

claim: GPT-5.5 dominates DeepSWE (70% pass rate) with 15+ point lead over Opus 4.7 (55%), contradicting other benchmarks that show them similar
actionable: For coding tasks, GPT-5.5 shows significantly better real-world performance than Opus 4.7 when properly evaluated
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...

claim: Claude Opus 4.7 uses 60k median output tokens vs GPT-5.5's 16k for the same tasks, costing 3x more per trial despite lower quality
actionable: When evaluating coding models, measure tokens-per-solve not just accuracy; GPT-5.5 is more efficient
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...

claim: Claude models miss stated multi-part requirements (e.g., support both sync and async) and forget to mirror changes across parallel branches
actionable: When prompting Claude for multi-part requirements, explicitly break them into separate steps and verify each is completed
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...

claim: GPT-5.5 reads prompts literally and produces patches honoring both prompt and repository contracts; Claude explores recent git history and sometimes bypasses specs
actionable: Use GPT-5.5 when you need deterministic behavior matching exact specs; Claude may be more exploratory
source_title: DeepSWE just changed the benchmark game...
